name: kiki
notes: Profile for the 'kiki' trained model. A profile encapsulates HOW this model must be used, so loading it 'just works' with no flags. pitch_shift=+12 is kiki's INHERENT setting -- kiki is a female model meant to be driven with the input transposed up an octave; it is applied automatically on load (verified to sound natural). index_rate=0.0, protect=0.33, f0_method=rmvpe. rms_mix_rate=1.0 keeps the model's own loudness (faithful). These match the original RVC tool kiki shipped with (mod/configs/config.json), which used pitch=+12, index_rate=0, f0=fcpe (we use rmvpe; infer_rvc_python's fcpe path is broken -- rmvpe is equivalent here), rms_mix_rate=0.5 (set 0.5 if you prefer source-loudness tracking). The --pitch CLI flag only overrides pitch per-run for experimentation; you never need to pass it. Paths are relative to the project root.
